data.epi_content:
There's nothing but laughter coming out.

Well, of course, it's absurd.

That I'm breathing and alive so perfectly fine.

This makes the absolute proposition I've been holding in my mind disappear.

That I contemplate my own death, and can let go of what's mine through free will whenever I want.

Well, I don't think I believed it so absolutely myself.

What was that shadowy figure I hazily saw in the bathtub?

I'm not particularly curious.

But, but. It's certain that it saved me.

My body shivers.

Sunlight passes through the window into the room, stinging my skin with heat.

With this much sunlight, my skin should have a slight reddish tint, but this pale skin doesn't seem to have any intention of changing.

When I impulsively stabbed my wrist in the heat of emotion, it didn't bother me at all.

But as the pain rushed in, and the trigger trying to escape that pain made a small mistake, some non-existent desire for life awakened, and now I'm breathing like this.

The wet bed, the smell of medicine, the smell of blood.

On the bed, red bloodstains have bloomed like flower patterns, scattered everywhere.

The smell. I hate it.

But why is my body so perfectly fine...

Growl.

Perhaps not so perfectly fine after all, as a sound came from my stomach.

"...Ah, I'm... hungry."

My throat is surprisingly dry.

So dry that my voice barely comes out.

I drank a lot of water while lying in the bathtub, unable to get up. Could it be because I lost too much blood?

But there's not a single wound visible on my body.

I touch my neck with my parched, dry hand, and needlessly stroke my intact wrist.

Not a single hole. How lewd.

What am I saying?

Looking around, it's my perfectly normal room.

I move my heavy feet toward the sink to get some water.

Did I sprain my leg while thrashing about in the bathtub?

My left leg is swollen red and hurts so much I nearly fall with each step.

Limping somehow to the sink, I turn on the water and start drinking.

I didn't even think of using a cup.

My throat was just burning with thirst.

I would have used one if I'd seen it, but it wasn't immediately visible.

My stomach hurts a bit from the cold water going straight in, but that's a secondary issue.

The main thing is, I'm alive.

Quite miraculously.

When I opened my eyes, I woke up in a blood-soaked bed, and except for being hungry and thirsty, my body seems quite fine.

For some reason I'm wearing the school uniform I had taken off, but maybe some pervert saved my life in exchange for playing dress-up with me.

Small, light, and cute-looking—I suppose I have quite a nice appearance for playing with like a doll.
data.epi_content2:
"Heheh."

I laughed at my own thoughts.

If someone saw me, they'd think I was completely insane.

Well, no matter what I speculate here, there doesn't seem to be anything I can figure out.

My skin feels sticky, so I should wash up first.

Since the uniform smells of blood too, I'll just wash with my clothes on.

They're already wet anyway.

I enter the bathroom.

The bathtub is clean.

There are no bloodstains, and no smell.

As a bonus, even the floor has been cleaned spotlessly, as if someone had cleaned it.

Come to think of it, I had placed the blade on the rack beside the bathtub.

They must have put that away too.

I wonder where they put the pistol.

Even though it's a firearm, it seems to be the only decent heirloom I have.

Why didn't I throw it away in the first place?

Was it because it was a gun?

Anyway, I turn on the water and wash my body starting from my head with hot water, and blood begins to flow down gently.

It feels like I'm doing something wrong.

Taking a shower with clothes on instead of taking them off to clean my body.

The way the clothes cling to me looks somewhat suggestive.

After thoroughly washing my body, I use magic I've learned to create hot air.

Since I don't know how to use magic to clean things, I just wet everything moderately and dry it with warm air.

Feeling slightly damp yet fluffy, I leave the bathroom barefoot.

A stinging sensation makes me look at my feet—a piece of broken glass is embedded in my foot.

I limp to the bed and roughly pull out the glass shard with my hand.

Maybe because a blade has already gone in and out of my wrist, or perhaps because I've already had a hole in my throat, it doesn't hurt much.

This sounds like I'm bragging.

Nothing looks as pathetic as a pathetic person bragging.

I notice a crumpled paper on the table.

I walk over and try to unfold it to check the contents, but someone has already scratched it heavily with their fingernails, leaving marks that cover the text and make it unreadable.

From the glimpse of the pattern and elegant lettering, it seems to be from my family.

Who would send it, when I have no money? Who could have sent it?

The half-wit or the fool. It must be one of those two.

If so, I don't need to bother reading it.

I sigh once and throw the paper back on the table, then return to the bed.

My body is too tired to do anything.

I feel like I might die soon even without any effort on my part.

Perhaps I'm in a coma right now and dreaming.

If possible, I'd like to have a lucid dream and stay forever in my own happy world.

As long as I don't shout that this place is a dream, the normal world will keep turning until I disappear.

I close my eyes slightly and open them again to see the sky, which had been screaming red before sunset, has mockingly turned black with the moon smiling brightly.

Looking at the clock, it's around 9 o'clock.
data.epi_content3:
And I'm still in my room.

Although the family portrait I tore up during one of my outbursts is hanging there, it's still my room.

The ornate plate with elaborate patterns that the traitor of our family gave me, which I once cherished, is still displayed on top of the wardrobe, unbroken.

Inside the wide-open closet are the glamorous dresses and various clothes I had hysterically torn to shreds.

Below them, the glass crafts that haven't been broken yet and the jewels that weren't thrown out the window or flushed down the toilet are still shining brightly.

It seems no one has been in and out of my room.

If someone had restored these things, there would have been some trace.

Or maybe they've opened my skull and are showing me hallucinations.

Right now, my head is filled with phrases that would fit on some movie promotional poster.

Where does reality end and fiction begin?

It's not a philosophical question like whether humans truly exist.

I really don't know what's what.

Well, thinking about it, it's all nonsense.

Living soaked in alcohol, then calling 1577-01... what was it? And what was the name of the facility? Too expensive for a mental hospital.

My memory is getting blurry.

Anyway, for example, if I called some poor unnamed counselor and said, "One morning I woke up and found myself as the villainous girl from a fallen noble family in a novel, and life was so painful and worthless that I was buried in thoughts of hopelessness, became pessimistic about the world, made an extreme choice, and then time rewound!"—who would believe that?

They'd think I was a mentally ill person talking nonsense, and tell me to have hope from some mental health center or suicide prevention center, and I'd say thanks for the help and hang up.

Shit. They might think I'm messing with them.

The problem is, there's no phone.

Ah, do I need to think about such complicated things?

It's not like thinking about it will help me solve anything.

Ha. What if dying means coming back again, such a cliché, such a...

Where is there such a trite, meaningless, terrible story that serves no purpose other than to torment me?

If that's the case, they shouldn't have told me this is a novel.

Then at least it might have been something in the suspense genre with a bit of thrill.

I look in the mirror again.

The girl looks perplexed.

Then eventually her expression relaxes and returns to the cold, expressionless face as always.

Her eyes, which had been filled with confusion and had a slight glimmer, slowly grow cold and lifeless again.

I'm hungry.

I open the drawer where I used to keep lots of chocolate, and sure enough, it's full of chocolate.

Even types of chocolate I hadn't seen since becoming this young lady.

And right next to it, I open the drawer that used to be half-filled with coffee grounds, and indeed it is.

Rather than thinking about whether I've returned or what's happening, I decide to satisfy my hunger first.

Though it feels more like a survival instinct than appetite, given how hungry I am.

The high-quality chocolate I haven't had in a long time, with a cup of coffee, was quite delightful.

It creates a sense of ease and brings a bit of happiness.

And soon that happiness shatters.

I didn't come back after dying.

I fell into an earlier time.

I realized this when I saw Evan standing in front of my door with an angry face.

That is, Evan wearing the same clothes, holding the same book, with the same expression as the day we fought.

And that day was the day I slapped Vivian's cheek.
